rationale: Keep: the citing note explicitly says this paper “respond[s] to Dhar, Menon, and Maach (2004)” and frames its contribution as extending KNS models to the same B2B/complex-buying issues raised there. The near-matching titles and shared focal constructs (compromise effect, complex buying contexts, group decision making, solution-centric offerings) indicate direct conceptual engagement rather than an incidental background mention.
